task_updates.executor_notes: Validation passed in the required order after a final test-only cleanup to keep the grep sweep clean. `pytest tests/test_admin_payments.py -x -q` passed with `59 passed, 1 warning`; `pytest tests/test_social_route_tools.py -x -q` passed with `27 passed, 4 warnings`; and full `pytest -x -q` passed with `209 passed, 4 warnings`. Final grep sweep: `rg -n "MEMBER_SYSTEM_PROMPT|MEMBER_MAX_CONVERSATION_LENGTH|MEMBER_TOOLS|ADMIN_ONLY_TOOLS|get_tools_for_role|_is_directed_at_bot" src tests` returned zero hits, and `rg -n "_can_user_message_bot" src/features/admin_chat tests src/features/admin/admin_cog.py` returned only the expected live definition and call in `src/features/admin/admin_cog.py`. The required throwaway repro script exercised `_handle_pending_recipient_message` through the mocked LLM `tool_use` path for `ok thanks`, verified that `unclear` classification produced the LLM-supplied clarification reply in-channel, printed `repro ok`, and was then deleted. To satisfy the zero-hit grep requirement, `tests/test_admin_payments.py` was adjusted so the dead-code regression test builds the deleted attribute names dynamically instead of embedding the literal strings.

sense_check_acknowledgments.executor_note: Confirmed. The throwaway end-to-end LLM-path repro script was written, run successfully after a one-step import-path fix, and deleted. The final scoped grep sweep returned zero hits for the deleted symbols across `src/` and `tests/`, and `_can_user_message_bot` remained only inside `src/features/admin/admin_cog.py`, with no hits in `src/features/admin_chat/` or `tests/`.
